item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure, but it may be due to a license incompatibility with creative commons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh hey, the image is gone, what happened with that?

item.title: Late +1
item.content:encoded:
I remember reading this back when it first came out and I think I didn't upvote then only because I wasn't signed up yet. Got linked back here from a newer post and upon rereading, I have to say, this piece is great and easily still holds up. My only (very minor) quibble would be the very end, since I simply don't understand the red screen of death. It feels like adding a completely new element to something that really just doesn't need it. That still isn't even close to being a problem for me overall, so here; please accept my belated +1.

Also have to say I love the little detail that it took Miami 26 replays to actually win one. Ha!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I WANT MOOORE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I haven't been on the wiki for a few years so I've been checking out some new entries and old favorites and I forgot how much I loved this one. The emotional impact of this one works so well for me. An entire arena full of strangers trapped in a groundhogs day scenario, except it's not even a full day, just a few hours being lived over and over and over again. Tensions really would flare up quickly. And with the knowledge that any harm done will be reset shortly, I can see the creepy religious people justifying their escalation to child sacrifice. Yikes. I want the Foundation to find a way to free them, but I guess as long as the video is never played again it is fine?

Anyway kudos for writing one of my favorite articles!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love "trapped in loops" situations too - and so this SCP, despite sounding lame at first, turned out to tap that wonderfully.

I've seen plenty of trapped/loop stories before but this one is interesting due to the unique location and especially creepy with the gradual realistation of the inhabitants of their situation. The hopelessness as you realise they are trapped for good, and are essentially now trapped in limbo as playing the disc again will just torture them even more. The final timeline entry of the red lights was a nice shudder moment as well.

What I find most interesting in these kind of scenarios is how people react. This one is done quite well although I think the ritual slaughter thing is a bit shlock horror. I'd like to see how things evolve over time in this world maybe with more plays of the disc or more accounts from the timeline.

The only thing that stood out to me as a fault in the article is there is a lot of dupliicated info between the article and the timeline attachment. By the time you get to the timeline, you already know about the events (aside from the red light one). It might be more effective to be more vague in the article and just refer to the timeline instead. I think the timeline is a simple and effective way to convey the horror and escalation over time (at least for the later entries, the first few episodes described in detail in the article is good because it gives you the background for how the remembering works).

PS If people like this kind of SCP, check out Stephen King's novel "Under The Dome" which features a town trapped within an impenetrable dome which appears out of nowhere
Happy to oblige. I've always loved this one. It's full of so much fridge horror.

What a wonderfully written article. Personally I'm a sucker for Groundhog Day-like stories so I might be a little biased. That said the horror was well done, and this definitely deserves a +1 from me.

We just need one more to 1000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is possibly my favorite SCP. I've been to several sports games (mostly hockey) in my lifetime, but this actually made the hair on the back of my neck stand up thinking about ending up "stuck" at one.

Also, I visualize the broadcast camera as one of those high-angle, up near the roof cameras, which makes this even creepier for me as I imagine the inhabitants being unaware of its presence. My mind also goes off the deep end imagining different events taking place, for example:

Infants/babies immediately crying uncontrollably at the beginning of broadcast, on account of their only memories being those they've retained from the playbacks.

The thought of that alone is terrifying. What do you remember from your early years? These kids have no memory of anything other than ritual murders and insanity, but perfect recollection of it happening however many different times, and all they can do is cry…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what'd happen if you used 3922 on this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ballers rise for the Foundation,keep it up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: cried a little the first time i read this…

This is unexpectedly creepy and great. +1

Edit: +900! Yay! This skip definitely deserves it. I want to see this reach +1000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know everyone here is mature enough to know what I mean when I say, I was really high when I read this. I was taking painkillers and resting from something and thought I'd read one SCP. Genuine nightmare fuel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This genuinely fucked me up for a couple of days in the way that only brilliant stories can.

Easily one of my favorites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's the big deal with Necroposting anyways, is it really THAT bad to respark an old debate?

It's fine to bring back old discussions so long as the person making the post is contributing something to the conversation (e.g., not just "lol" or "I agree" or "that's dumb") in a meaningful way that's not just calling someone out on an old post or bugging someone for a response after years have gone by.

Further reading: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-594642/necro-posting-and-you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd come to the conclusion that I should break all the cameras and see what happens. I looked at the date AFTER I posted it like a tardnugget, so this is a 5 year necropost, lol. Oh well. What's the big deal with Necroposting anyways, is it really THAT bad to respark an old debate?

item.title: Just a bunch of stuff from my braineus maximus
item.content:encoded:
The typical "Religious dudes go crazy and start sacrificing crap" you see oh so often in Hollywood kinda irked me a bit(it obviously wasn't your intention, just thought I'd mention that as no one else has), but other than that bit it's a super interesting idea. If anything, the more logical solution is that the Boston Churchgoers would get angry at God/lose their faith or something like that, rather than suddenly deciding to sacrifice their electronics to these random gods they just made up and proceed to butcher people.They didn't even do normal religious person stuff, the storyline was kinda: figuring stuff out, massive orgy, then BAM sarificial keychain baby murder. Also, why are the parents OK with the Faithkeepers sacrificing Lebron James and that other dude in front of their children, but when they want to sacrifice the children they suddenly get all defensive? On another note, You should make a tale or an Experiment Log where the Foundation tries to break the endless loop in some way shape or form. You could also try and add a little Addendum or something with the Keynote "Never expose to SCP-682 under any circumstances. Lord only knows what will happen, and frankly, I want to keep it that way."
I know I'm just throwing a bunch of crap at you all at once, so forgive me for that, lol, I just kept thinking of stuff and slapped it on my original comment. Here, have a doot doot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, bbaztek made a bunch up updates to this and the Sopranos one in 2015, and then launched a campaign to ensure he wouldn't be around for awhile, for inexplicable reasons. Unfortunate, because he was a good author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe this was added way after the article was written. When I first read it back in 2013, it wasn't there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First recorded deviation in arena light to a deep red color. [DATA EXPUNGED].

Love that this is the last entry, leaves it open-ended as to what happens next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that last playback

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote. Excellent execution.

item.title: Re: New Playback
item.content:encoded:
It's absurd that the last scene is the camera happening to have a red tint from the start. So what's going on? There needs to be a new chapter here, and it would be harmless to explore.

UPvote withheld

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Go read this tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My favorite SCP.
Would love it if it was expanded

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure why

I remember this SCP because it is fucking horrifying. That is pretty subjective, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason this SCP stuck with me. I spent some time away from this website but I always remembered this SCP. Not sure why

item.title: Re: Hmm... the cameras
item.content:encoded: I apologize for necroposting, but, since no one else seems to have noticed your post, I need to point out that this is a brilliant idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First recorded deviation in arena light to a deep red color. [DATA EXPUNGED].

Deep red colour you say?

Also, speaking of ritual murders and metafiction...

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me so uncomfortable. I like the new entry about the red light (which wasn't there before, I think?) ; it gives the article just a little bit more story that especially makes it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something hilarious about one of the group throwing an orgy to shut the religious fanatics down.

item.title: My favorite SCP
item.content:encoded: I'm brand new to this site, and this is great inspiration to me. Well done author! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
However, to note, no entities inside SCP-1733 have ever addressed the viewer directly, or that they reside in a digital recording.

This sentence feels to me like it's missing something. Maybe the phrase "displayed awareness" before the word "that."

Overall, a very good portrayal of the dawning awareness that you're in a time loop, and how awful that would be. I like that the Foundation has discontinued playbacks because it's too cruel to the imprisoned consciousnesses — if it helped them understand the anomaly better, I'm sure they'd still do it, but as it is any further playback would just be pointless torture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The biggest question I have with this skip is how what's being shown in the page image is supposed to be different from what actually happened. Did some roughhousing happen?

Also, some food for thought from Max Landis and friends.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if one doesn't make a full playthrough. What if someone stops and rewinds it in the middle?
In the case of a copy, this could be what sets the original and the copy appart.
I suspcet, the entities within one of them would not have recollection of that missing part of the playthrough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hexaflange, don't touch my SCP.

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Revision 51 has been reverted back to Revision 50 due to deleting an entry from the playback log without stated permission in the edit comments or in the discussion thread. See the Rules under editing. If you wish to make substantial edits, please contact the author. This acts an official warning for the user editing in question. Continuing to do so will result in further disciplinary action.

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Revision 49 has been reverted back to Revision 48 due to deleting an entry from the playback log without stated permission in the edit comments or in the discussion thread. See the Rules under editing. If you wish to make substantial edits, please contact the author.

item.title: Re: New Playback
item.content:encoded: The playback number of the last entry should really be changed back to three digits- otherwise I think it's fine.

item.title: New Playback
item.content:encoded:
I'm a bit disappointed with the new entry, but I suppose that's at least partly because I read it before the addition.

-snip- Addressed; Thank you, author!

As for the content of the playback, the first part I'm kinda in the middle with but I can understand. It shifts away the previous main focus of the people// dealing with this to the person of interest/human sacrifices, which is valid albeit a bit unnecessary. The expungement, however, leaves a real bad taste in my mouth. Though this is partially because I am a grumpy gus and hate change, I feel it doesn't have build up. The sentence before it tilts it towards the demonic aspect, and that just makes me think the expungement is of generic demonic shenanigans; meanwhile, if the sentence before it was absent, the reader would be free to either think of ramifications of the human sacrifice or that the people did something really, really fucked up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just read this for the first time. I think that the final playback message made the difference between a high neutral and a solid upvote for me.

Without the final playback, this is an interesting concept slightly bogged down by the means of discovery, slow opening to the description, and then tell/show issues with describing the progression of the playbacks twice.

With the final playback, moving the number of playbacks from 3 to 7 digits is very suggestive of the black depths of horror, despair and ennui that the crowd must be going through, and the change in light colour alludes nicely to the creation of otherworldly conditions within the reality of the recording whilst leaving the details to the imagination. I am very much in favour.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had a laugh at the end, when they resorted to ritual murder. Nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit, the human sacrifices started to actually work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not blood on the camera. Look at the playback number. Foundation kept pushing it and now something Very Bad has happened

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ending the article with expungement was enough to drop my upvote to neutral. It doesn't need to end with the foundation decided not to continue testing to save children but to get my vote back to would need to end in something more interesting then expungement

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I don't know if it is an improvement. I can see that he is trying to give it a more finalized ending, but I am not convinced it is really needed. On the other hand it isn't going to change my vote either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Narrative-wise though, it felt so… Horrifying and incredible to end on that final video, people demanding sacrifices and adults trying to protect their children.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Oops, disregard. Didn't see this wasn't an open post. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Blood on the cameras, is probably the intent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ditto on the red lighting. Makes you wonder what happened now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see the language being tightened up, though I did like that the final playback recorded was about the Proselytizers wanting to sacrifice the children. It ended the article on such a good note that I'm wary about the additional playback of the lighting changing to another color because [EXPUNGEMENT].

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Page Revisions 42 + 43 have been reverted for changing a unit of force and the number of units without apparent reason or permission from the author. If you wish to make more than minor changes to an article, please contact the original author. See the rules under "Editing".

item.title: Re: Contaimnent procedures
item.content:encoded: Site numbers are an exception to the normal 'no redaction in containment procedures' rule, the logic being that anyone involved in containment is presumably already at the site and doesn't need to be told where they are.

item.title: Contaimnent procedures
item.content:encoded:
Site-██

Why you censored it, neutral vote until changes.

item.title: Requesting permission for one final experiment
item.content:encoded:
I would honestly love to submit one last further experiment provided it's approved, or as a spin-off tale later on with more detail if not. This last test will also align the description of SCP-1733 ending with "testing has been suspended indefinitely" as the last noted playback only states "testing suspended at this point."

Playback ███ Permission granted for further experimentation by ██████ ████████. DVR was connected to DVD-Video recorder and VCR in tandem to assess whether recordings of the recording are also affected and the degree. During DVR playback proselytizers are able to successfully kidnap a child causing a small riot near Section 319, ██ adults and █ children are either severely harmed or killed in the altercation. Once playback has concluded, the DVD-Video disc and VHS created are reviewed independently. On initial playback of the DVD-Video copy, the subjects within the stadium still retain cognizance from previous playbacks on DVR but are slightly disoriented once again, and are forced to reassess their situation as per previous DVR playbacks, members of the crowd further in the back are affected the greatest. Second play through of DVD-Video reveals cognizance has not improved, nearly identical playback to first. On initial playback of the VHS copy, the subjects appear to have lost the majority of their cognizance and the teams emerge dazed and confused, the crowd displaying symptoms as well. The majority of playback is spent with commentators discussing a possible illness afflicting everyone at the game while medical staff assess players, staff, and the crowd. Members of the audience far in the back of the stadium and almost unseen on the VHS are quickly found to be husks of human remains, panic ensues and the recording ends prematurely with the stadium set ablaze from off-screen. DVD-Video disc has been destroyed in compliance with protocol ████, and VHS copy also destroyed in compliance with protocol ██. Further testing has been suspended indefinitely.

Just offering an expansion, something to line up the end of the playback logs with the description and explore it just a little further.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved this, had a few thoughts on furthering/focusing it.

1.) Given time, I agree that they would probably exhaust themselves and turn again to reason. At some point they might discover that their loop corresponds to the length of the scheduled broadcast. This would lead to them gaining some awareness that the broadcast/recording is part of their prison, and they might try to communicate with the outside world. I don't think the Foundation would ever be able to talk back or help them, but they would be trapped guessing that someone was watching them suffer.

2.) I like the bit about it going to a single camera when everyone else abandons their post. This could be the source of the anomaly. A single TV camera at the back of the stadium, that no one remembers setting up, with a Factory logo on the bottom of it…

3.) I just love this idea:
MBelinkie: "So even as everyone descends into anarchy, the anarchy is still covered by 18 cameras in crisp HD, with slow motion instant replays."
And I am probably going to write a tale where they gain sentience but are still forced to go through the motions of a "game". So the screaming, discussions, fights, murders, would all have to be carried out by the players, planned by the coaches, and commented on by the sportscasters to the cheers of the crowd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, it's marvelous. It sets out to be what an SCP should be in my opinion. It makes you ponder what would you do in that situation, it makes you look into the abyss so-to-speak and have it stare back to you as my main fear has always been being stripped of my own existential choices. Sure, I probably would've tried for a more scientific route rather than eventually lead into a cult like every lovecraft book I've ever read. But. It's done so realistically that you can kind of feel the audience stare back at you, figuratively speaking.

I'm reminded of an interesting comic that said nightmares happen when dreams realize they exist and have agency only for the moment that you sleep. It's pure horror and I cannot upvote this enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A'right, it gets silly at times (the foundation taking a Facebook argument seriously and BEING RIGHT, the people somehow turning to ritualistic murders) but it's written well enough that it feels juuuust plausible enough to not ruin suspension of disbelief.

I also appreciate the fact that the SCP comes from no identifiable source, it seems to have just randomly happened, which reinforces its otherworldly aspect, as well as the fact that the researchers suspended testing at the point when child sacrifice was bout to come into play, showing that the Foundation DOES have standards (I always hate it when people have them be evil for evil's sake).

I like this. A lot. Video SCPs seem to bring out the best in writers. +1 from me.

I'd like to know if they ever tried stopping the playback prematurely though. I figure the article would mention if they did. Would only the early parts be affected, then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed to that. I have to add that I was not aware anyone else used the phrase "Gods above and below."
